user_query:
I need to clean up and update our weekly progress documentation based on the current code, config, and latest ablation results. Please do the following and produce two artifacts:

Inputs to use:
- Draft report: docs/progress_report_draft.md
- Algorithm code: src/feature_selector.py
- Default hyperparameters: config/defaults.yaml
- Ablation results: data/ablation_runs.csv

Deliverables:
1) Save an updated report to output/progress_report_updated.md that:
   - Corrects the Algorithm section to state the actual algorithm name from the code and the worst-case time complexity exactly as written in the code docstring.
   - Replaces the Hyperparameters list with the current default values from config/defaults.yaml. Include these keys: selection_method, max_features, stopping_threshold, random_state, normalize. Show each as "key: value" on its own line.
   - Updates the Results section by computing the average val_f1 for each config_variant in data/ablation_runs.csv (baseline and tuned). Round each average to two decimals and also report the delta (tuned - baseline) rounded to two decimals.
   - Removes all TODO markers and bracketed tags (e.g., "[TODO]") from the draft while keeping the remaining sentence text intact.
   - Appends a "Changelog" section at the end with bullet points for exactly these items: algorithm name corrected; worst-case complexity updated from code; hyperparameter defaults synced from config; results averages and delta recomputed from CSV.

2) Compose a concise status summary and save it to output/status_summary.md with:
   - Title line: "Weekly Status: Feature Selector".
   - A line with "Algorithm: <name>" using the algorithm name from src/feature_selector.py.
   - A line with "Version: <version>" using the __version__ value from src/feature_selector.py.
   - A "Defaults:" section listing each default from config/defaults.yaml on its own line as "key: value" (same five keys as above).
   - A single "Results:" line of the form: "baseline avg F1: <X>; tuned avg F1: <Y>; delta: <Z>" using the two-decimal averages and delta you computed from the CSV.
   - A "Blockers:" line that is "none" if tuned avg F1 >= baseline avg F1, otherwise "performance regression".

Notes:
- Do not change headings other than adding the required "Changelog" to the report.
- Use only the provided local files for facts and numbers.
- Ensure both output files are created under the output/ directory exactly as named above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
